stimulus: A simulation developed by ecologist Hana Park projects that the rate of pollination—the rate at which pollen is transferred between flowers—in a meadow situated approximately 8 kilometers from the nearest forest edge ______ roughly double the rate of pollination in a meadow only 800 meters from the forest edge.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The convention being tested is finite and nonfinite verb forms within a sentence. Relative clauses, such as the one beginning with "that," require a finite verb to serve as the main verb of the clause. This choice correctly supplies the clause with the finite future tense verb "will be."

Choice B is incorrect because the nonfinite participle "being" does not supply the clause with a finite verb. Choice C is incorrect because the nonfinite to-infinitive "to be" does not supply the clause with a finite verb. Choice D is incorrect because the nonfinite to-infinitive "to have been" does not supply the clause with a finite verb.
